instructions:
## AutoDS Products Research API — Agent Reference

Read-only product-discovery endpoints. Every operation here is side-effect-free.

### Common patterns
- `product_id` in a URL is the 24-character hex id string of a research/winning product (e.g. obtained from `get_winning_products`).
- The caller's currency and region are resolved server-side from the user's settings — request bodies/params do NOT need (and cannot override) `region`.
- Pagination is `offset` (>=0) + `limit`; `get_winning_products` caps `limit` at 100.
- `get_product_by_id` may return HTTP 307 with a redirect message when the product is a winning product the user lacks the Product Finding Hub add-on to view — this is a documented business response, not a failure.

### Search filters (search_products body)
`filters` is a list of `{name, value, value_type, op}` objects (all four fields required, all strings). `order_by` is `{name, direction}` and is required by the upstream. Useful filter ops: `=`, `ne`, `in`, `between`, `>`, `<`, `search`. Example free-text search: `{"name": "search_query", "value": "wireless earbuds", "value_type": "string", "op": "search"}`.

### Winning-products query
- `sort` is a field name; prefix with `-` for descending (e.g. `-created_at` for newest first, `-orders_count`).
- `profit` / `item_cost` are range buckets (e.g. `10-20`, `100-` for 100+); `created_date` is a day-window enum (0=today, 1=yesterday, 7, 14, 30).
- `ads` filters by advertising platform name (e.g. `Facebook`, `TikTok`); comma-separated for multiple.

operations.summary: Search the product-research catalog by filter.
operations.notes: Read-only POST. Searches the product-research catalog and returns {results: [...], currency, region}. Winning products and deleted products are excluded automatically. The body is a filter object: `order_by` ({name, direction}) is REQUIRED; `filters` is an optional list of {name, value, value_type, op}. Free-text search is a filter with name=search_query, op=search, value_type=string. `region` is resolved from the user's settings server-side and is ignored if sent. Use `offset`/`limit` for pagination (default limit 10).

body_schema.description: Product-research search filter. `order_by` is required by the upstream.

name.description: Field to sort by, e.g. created_at.

filters.description: Filter conditions combined per `condition`. Omit for an unfiltered listing.

name.description: Filterable field name, e.g. search_query, rating, variations.variation_details.price.

value.description: Operand value (always a string; numeric/bool values are cast per value_type).

operations.summary: List winning products with optional discovery filters.
operations.notes: Read-only. Returns {results: [...]} of winning products. `offset`, `limit` (1-100) and `sort` are required. `sort` is a field name; prefix with `-` for descending (e.g. -created_at, -orders_count). Optional filters: category (24-character hex id), ads (advertising platform name, e.g. Facebook/TikTok), orders (min order count), profit / item_cost (range buckets like 10-20 or 100-), created_date (day window: 0=today,1,7,14,30), search (free text). Without the Product Finding Hub subscription the service returns a limited free set.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort field; prefix with '-' for descending, e.g. -created_at.

parameters.name: ads
parameters.description: Advertising platform name(s), comma-separated, e.g. Facebook,TikTok.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Free-text search across title, description, or supplier.

operations.summary: Get a single product by its id.
operations.notes: Read-only. Returns the full product document for the given product id (24-character hex string), with prices/region normalised to the user's settings. May return HTTP 307 with a message when the product is a winning product the user can't view without the Product Finding Hub add-on — a documented business response.

operations.summary: Get products similar to the given product.
operations.notes: Read-only. Returns {results: [...]} of products similar to the given product id (same region/categories, excluding winning and deleted products), preferring the same private supplier. Up to ~20 results.

parameters.name: product_id
parameters.description: Product id (24-character hex string) to find similar products for.

operations.summary: Get products recommended for the given product.
operations.notes: Read-only. Returns {results: [...], total_results} of products recommended for the given product id, with prices normalised to the user's currency/region. Use `offset`/`limit` for pagination (default limit 10).

parameters.name: product_id
parameters.description: Product id (24-character hex string) to base recommendations on.
